fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: N. Dakota Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in North Dakota — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license north dakota, dental assistant certification north dakota, RDA north dakota, DANB CDA north dakota, dental assistant radiography north dakota

board.name: North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in North Dakota.

steps.title: Create an Online Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the ND Board of Dental Examiners Online Portal.

steps.title: Complete the Application
steps.description: Log in to the ND Board of Dental Examiners Online Portal, select the 'Registered Dental Assistant' application, and complete all required sections, including uploading a current CPR certification card.

steps.title: Fulfill Educational/Experience & Exam Requirements
steps.description: Ensure official verification of graduation from a CODA-accredited program or two years of full-time clinical experience is sent, and have DANB send your Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) exam results directly to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Jurisprudence Exam & Background Check
steps.description: Successfully complete the North Dakota State Jurisprudence Exam (available within the portal) and submit fingerprints for the required criminal background check (CBC).

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $75 through the online portal.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor
steps.description: Submit your completed application and monitor its status via the ND Board of Dental Examiners Online Portal. The Board will notify you upon completion of review and verification of all supporting documents.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

faqs.question: Does North Dakota require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, North Dakota requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in North Dakota?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in North Dakota.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in North Dakota?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in North Dakota?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in North Dakota
hero.dek: To become a Registered Dental Assistant in North Dakota, apply online through the ND Board of Dental Examiners Online Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.portal_name: ND Board of Dental Examiners Online Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the ND Board of Dental Examiners Online Portal and click 'New User Registration' to create an account. Once registered, log in to access the application forms.
